title: Format Model Citations
description: Parse and format source citations from model output.
problem: The model returns answers with inline citations like [1], [2]. Write format_citations(answer, sources) where sources is a list of strings, that appends a numbered references section at the end. Only include sources that are actually cited.
starter_code:
import re

def format_citations(answer, sources):
    # Find all [n] citations in answer
    # Build a References section with only cited sources
    # Return answer + '\n\nReferences:\n' + numbered list
    return answer  # fix this
hint: Use re.findall(r'\[(\d+)\]', answer) to get cited numbers. Convert to ints, deduplicate, sort. Build the references block.

test_cases.label: Single citation
test_cases.expected:
See [1] for details.
